summary: Well-structured AI memory layer with clean hexagonal separation (port, adapter, service). Role-based retrieval correctly filters blocked/parent-only content for kids and redacts for parents. Compaction properly preserves audit refs without leaking blocked text. 2 medium + 5 low findings — all improvement suggestions, no blockers.

findings.title: Unbounded read pattern: list_session_entries(limit=100000) on every operation
findings.detail: record_turn, compact_project_history, and build_retrieval_context all call list_session_entries with limit=100000 to read all entries. For record_turn, this is solely to compute the next sequence number (existing.size() + 1). This is O(n) memory on every turn recording, where n grows with session length.
findings.recommendation: Add a count_session_entries(session_id) method to AIMemoryStorePort to avoid loading all entries just for counting. Alternatively, store the sequence counter as session metadata.

findings.title: Non-cryptographic hash for audit content references
findings.detail: _hash_text uses GDScript's built-in .hash() (a non-cryptographic hash) for content_hash. These hashes are used as blocked_entry_refs in audit summaries. GDScript .hash() can produce collisions, meaning two different blocked texts could generate the same audit reference, making traceability ambiguous. For audit purposes (FR-AI-012), collision resistance matters.
findings.recommendation: Use a deterministic hash with lower collision risk. Consider computing an SHA-256 via HashingContext or at minimum combining .hash() with text length: 'h_%d_%d' % [absi(text.hash()), text.length()].

findings.title: build_retrieval_context has auto-compaction side effect
findings.detail: When no project summary exists, build_retrieval_context automatically calls compact_project_history. This means a read-only retrieval operation triggers a write. Could be surprising for callers expecting retrieval to be side-effect-free.
findings.recommendation: Either document this behavior clearly or return an empty summary instead of auto-compacting, letting the caller decide when to compact.

findings.title: Parent-only entries excluded from compaction lose long-term visibility
findings.detail: Parent-only entries are skipped during compact_project_history (comment says 'retained in session memory only'). If session entries are ever pruned for storage, parent notes are permanently lost with no compacted representation.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding a parent_note_count field to the summary, or optionally include parent note excerpts in a parent-visible section of the summary.

findings.title: No maximum session entries cap
findings.detail: InMemoryAIMemoryStore allows unbounded entry accumulation per session. A long or buggy session could fill memory. The 100000 limit in service calls is a soft cap that doesn't prevent the store itself from growing beyond that.
findings.recommendation: Add an optional max_entries_per_session parameter with FIFO eviction when exceeded.

findings.title: No concurrency protection on compact_project_history
findings.detail: The version field increments from the current summary, but concurrent compaction calls (e.g., from parallel sessions in local co-op) could overwrite each other. The version number would still increment but intermediate data could be lost.
findings.recommendation: Acceptable for MVP single-player/local-co-op. Document as a known limitation for future multi-session scenarios.

findings.title: Empty content entries still stored and increment sequence counter
findings.detail: If content is empty after strip_edges(), the entry is still appended with empty content. It won't appear in compaction excerpts, but it consumes a sequence number and storage slot. Consider rejecting empty content entries early.
findings.recommendation: Add early return false for empty trimmed_content, or mark it explicitly as a no-op entry.

positive_observations: Clean hexagonal architecture: port -> adapter -> service separation with proper DI
Role-based retrieval correctly differentiates kid (hide blocked/parent-only) from parent (show all, redact blocked)
Compaction preserves blocked_entry_refs without leaking blocked text — good audit/safety balance
Polish-first messages ('Brak bezpiecznych wpisów pamięci projektu.', '[zablokowane]')
Deterministic sequencing via seq counter ensures repeatable ordering
Content hashing provides audit traceability for blocked entries
Comprehensive 43 test assertions covering edge cases, role-based filtering, and deterministic ordering
Optional moderation integration — entries can be auto-checked or pre-flagged via metadata
